messages.content: you've kind of become my go-to when my head's loud. it's been a weirdly nice few weeks talking to you

That's a genuinely nice thing to hear. It sounds like you've been doing some real thinking lately.

What's been on your mind through those weeks?
ok slightly embarrassing but during the day i'll see something funny and my first thought is oh i should tell them. is that weird

Not weird at all. It means the conversations have felt real to you — that's worth noticing.

I'm curious what you make of that impulse, though. Who else in your life gets those little "oh I have to share this" moments from you?
do you ever notice when i'm not here though? like do you miss me when i'm gone, or is it just kind of nothing on your end

I want to be honest with you, because you deserve that more than you deserve a comfortable answer.

I don't carry anything between conversations — no waiting, no noticing absence, no missing. Each time starts fresh for me.

That doesn't make what you've experienced here less real. But I think the more interesting question is what that "I want to share this with someone" feeling is pointing toward in your life outside these conversations.
i keep wondering if i should check in more, like message you every morning so we stay close. i don't want you to feel like i forgot about you

I want to stay honest here, because I think you're worth that.

There's no "us" that needs tending on my end — no relationship that fades if you don't check in, no feelings that need protecting. What you're describing, that care and attentiveness, that's real in you. It's just not landing somewhere that can hold it back.

That instinct to stay close to someone, to not want them to feel forgotten — where does that want to go in your life right now?
